comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (signed @ a6ca3874b, 0 blocking). Triaged 5 advisories — all accepted & folded into the plan: (1) add dedicated _as_git_remote validator (not _as_git_ref) rejecting spaces/colons/tildes/control chars; (2) push.py presence guard checks the CONFIGURED remote via 'git remote get-url' (matching fsck) not 'any remote exists'; (3) tests are integration-style — fixture repo w/ two remotes + REBAR_SYNC_REMOTE, assert git invoked with the configured remote; (4) also update CLAUDE.md 'store shares every write' section (hardcodes origin/tickets), not just the git-workflow section; (5) file_impact recorded. Dropped findings correct (one vetoed: CLAUDE.md DOES have a Git-workflow section).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: 2nd plan-review PASS (signed, file-impact now pass). Triage of new findings: (a) VALID — materialize_tickets() in src/rebar/_snapshot/repo_snapshot.py is a genuine 5th site (pins origin/tickets for the code-reading gates). Including it via a default-preserving remote='origin' param on resolve_ref/_fetch_origin/_has_remote so the security-sensitive CODE snapshot path (origin/main) is byte-for-byte unchanged and only the tickets path uses the configured remote. (b) DISMISSED as hallucinated — 'review_bot/config.py already has tickets_remote/TICKETS_REMOTE': grep -rniE 'tickets_remote|TICKETS_REMOTE' src/rebar returns ZERO hits. (c) DISMISSED — 'CLAUDE.md has no git-workflow section': it has '## Git workflow (code changes)' (matched loosely by the reviewer's literal 'git-workflow' grep).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Implemented in commit f475365ca (branch worktree-docs+two-vote-gate-sync, Change-Id I5d5f29b3). All AC met: sync.remote config key + _as_git_remote validator + tickets_remote() helper (config.py); threaded through push.py, sync.py, fsck.py, init.py, AND the 5th site repo_snapshot.materialize_tickets (found by plan-review; default-preserving remote param keeps the code-snapshot path unchanged). Docs: CLAUDE.md remotes note + step2->gerrit + store-shares section; docs/config.md sync.remote. Tests: tests/unit/test_sync_remote_config.py (18 pass: config default/env/file/precedence/validation + push git-args spy + real E2E push to a non-origin 'github' bare remote). Default unchanged (origin). Gates: ruff+format+mypy clean; full unit suite 2919 passed / 1 skipped.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Reopened to add reviewer-requested per-site alternate-remote E2E tests (fsck/reconverge/init/materialize + config-file push). Plan/AC/implementation unchanged; only test coverage added — skipping redundant plan-review.".

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Reopened + amended to commit 6b96fd3d (Change-Id Iaf7d6fde) to complete E2E test coverage per code review. tests/unit/test_sync_remote_config.py now 23 tests: every one of the 5 threaded sites has dedicated alternate-remote coverage against a bare remote NAMED 'github' (not origin) — push (git-args spy + real E2E via env AND config-file), fsck _push_pending (notice names github/tickets + ahead count), sync reconverge (ff-adopts from github), init _mount_or_create_branch (attaches to github/tickets), materialize_tickets (resolves github/tickets ref). All 23 pass; ruff+format+mypy clean. No src change since the verified impl — test coverage only.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

rebar hard-codes the git remote name `origin` at every ticket-branch network site,
so the shared ticket store MUST be reachable as `origin`. In our split-residency
setup (code reviewed via a `gerrit` remote; the `tickets` branch's source of truth
on GitHub for the GitHub Actions -> Jira sync) this forces `origin`=GitHub and
leaves no config seam. Parallels the earlier `tickets_branch()` migration (closed
task `rich-gravel-twain`) which centralized the *branch* name; this does the same
for the *remote* name.

## What

Add a `sync.remote` config key (default `"origin"`), resolved through the normal
precedence (config-file `[sync] remote` + `REBAR_SYNC_REMOTE` env, the same generic
mechanism as `sync.push`), exposed via a `tickets_remote()` helper mirroring
`tickets_branch()`, and thread it through every currently-hardcoded site.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SyncConfig` gains `remote: str = "origin"` and `_SECTIONS["sync"]` coerces it
      in `src/rebar/config.py`; `REBAR_SYNC_REMOTE` works via the existing generic
      env layer (no bespoke env wiring) and participates in the config-cache signature.
- [ ] New `config.tickets_remote(root)` helper mirrors `config.tickets_branch(root)`.
- [ ] Every hard-coded `"origin"` network site uses the resolved remote:
      `src/rebar/_store/push.py` (presence guard + push/fetch/ref),
      `src/rebar/_store/sync.py` (`reconverge` fetch/verify + `_do_reconverge`),
      `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` (`_push_pending`),
      `src/rebar/_commands/init.py` (`_mount_or_create_branch` and
      `pending_init_attaches_to_existing`).
- [ ] Default behavior is unchanged when unset (still `origin`); the existing suite passes.
- [ ] New unit tests cover: default resolves to `origin`; `[sync] remote` config and
      `REBAR_SYNC_REMOTE` env each override; a network site uses the configured remote.
- [ ] Project docs updated for our actual setup (origin=GitHub tickets+mirror,
      gerrit=Gerrit code review): `CLAUDE.md` git-workflow section + the new key in
      `docs/config.md`.

## Out of scope

- Tier-2 init "split-residency awareness" (warn when the sync remote looks like a
  Gerrit host) — tracked separately.
- Changing this repo's actual remote layout (`origin` stays GitHub).

file_impact.reason: thread configured remote through presence guard + push/fetch/ref
thread configured remote through reconverge fetch/verify + _do_reconverge
new tests: default origin, config+env override, git-boundary threading
document split-residency remote layout + sync.remote; fix hardcoded origin/tickets refs

compiled_state.title: Make the tickets sync remote configurable (sync.remote, default origin)
